A lot of Banshees come from the factory with the positive wire (orange) on the [-] terminal and the ground wire (black) on the [+] terminal of the coil; switching the wires would put orange on [+] and black on [-]

cells.formula: =IF(AND(C91>=-30,C91<=-1),"Sizes larger on the mains",IF(AND(C91>=0,C91<=29),"Sizes larger on the mains",IF(AND(C91>=30,C91<=59),"Size larger on the mains",IF(AND(C91>=60,C91<=90),"No Change",IF(AND(C91>=91,C91<=120),"Size smaller on the mains",IF(AND(C91>=121,C91<=150),"Sizes smaller on the mains",IF(AND(C91>=151,C91<=180),"Sizes smaller on the mains","")))))))

cells.value: 1)   Gap 2 new spark plugs at 0.7-0.8 mm (0.028-0.031 in)
2)   Warm-up the motor to normal operating temp on the old plugs
3)   Remove the old plugs (careful of the hot pipes)
4)   Mark the new plug boxes "R" and "L"
5)   Put the old plugs in the new plug boxes
7)   Accellerate through all the gears into top gear with the throttle pinned
8)   As soon as you hit the top of top gear, hit the kill switch, pull-in the clutch, and hit the brakes
9)   Remove the spark plugs and ride back on the old plugs
10)  Put the new plug, vertically, upsidedown into a vise and use a hacksaw to cut the threads away from the center electrode
a)  Cut from the bottom of the plug towards the top, resting the blade on the porcelain
b)  Once you've bottomed out on the base, reposition the plug horizontally in the vise
c)  Cut from the side at the base until both cuts meet and the threaded section falls
11)  You're looking for a 2 mm brown ring at the base of the white porcelain meaning the main jets are dialed in perfectly
a)  If the ring is darker than chocolate brown or taller than 2mm, you're rich on the mains, but if it revs clean to full throttle, you're good
b)  If the ring is smaller than 2 mm or lighter than chocolate brown, you're lean; go 1 larger on the mains and try again
